commands.hypothesis: v42 cohort - breakeven-stop DPM sweep on the v25-bot-excl-climbing universe. All 5 mirrors share v25 base (TP=100/SL=30, entry@T+60, 5s poll, no trailing TP, filters: top5<=30 + dd>-25 + vel<200 + age>=60 + climbing_t30_t45=1). Only breakevenStopPct varies across {10, 15, 20, 30, 40}. Mechanic (position-manager.ts ~line 481): once the position's high-water-mark reaches entry x (1 + breakevenStopPct/100), the SL floor is raised to the entry price. A token that pumps past the activation level then reverses exits at ~scratch (0% gross, slightly negative net after gap penalty + slippage costs) instead of the full -30% SL. This directly attacks the dominant loser pattern observed in loss-postmortem: trades that briefly pump then crash to a -30% SL fire (e.g. grad 23115 entered +70%, grad 23283 entered +35% - both touched positive territory before the rug). CAVEAT (note for future sessions): pure breakeven locks in NO profit, only protects capital - it converts would-be SL losses into scratches. The cost is false exits: trades that wiggle up to the activation level, dip back to entry (breakeven fires), then recover to TP - those become scratch exits instead of wins. Lower activation (10) = more protection + more false exits; higher activation (40) = less of both. Goal per the operator: lock in gains when a token at least moves up, by removing the give-it-all-back tail. Success = a breakeven level that lifts total_net_sol AND drop_top3 above the v25 control (+4.95 / +2.64 at n=352) by cutting the catastrophic SL tail faster than it sacrifices recoveries. If ALL levels underperform v25, the false-exit cost dominates and breakeven is net-negative on this universe (the v25-bot-excl-breakeven experiment at breakevenStopPct=20 previously went -0.41 SOL on n=86 - this cohort retests across a wider activation range with the now-larger v25 baseline).

Breakeven stop activates at +10% HWM. Most protective / earliest-engaging variant - the SL moves to entry as soon as the token is up 10%. Hypothesis: catches the largest share of pumped-then-dumped losers, converting many -30% SL fires into scratches. Risk: +10% is well inside the normal noise band (v25 p75 net is +26.7%), so a large fraction of eventual winners will wiggle to +10%, dip to entry, get stopped at breakeven, then run to TP without us - the highest false-exit rate of the cohort. Expected: lowest SL count, but also lowest TP count. Net effect is the open question - this is the variant most likely to over-protect.

Breakeven stop activates at +15% HWM. Slightly later engagement than the +10 variant - trades the marginal extra protection for fewer false exits. Tests whether the +10 to +15 step meaningfully reduces the false-exit rate while still catching most pump-then-dump losers. Expected to sit between v42-breakeven-10 and v42-breakeven-20 on both SL-reduction and false-exit cost.

Breakeven stop activates at +20% HWM. Direct re-test of the prior v25-bot-excl-breakeven experiment (breakevenStopPct=20, which went -0.41 SOL on n=86 and was deprioritized). Re-running on the now-larger/stronger v25 baseline (n=352, +4.95 SOL) to confirm or refute. +20% is just below the v25 p75 (+26.7%), so this activation only fires on trades that reach the upper quartile of the move distribution - should have a much lower false-exit rate than the +10/+15 variants while still protecting the bigger movers that reverse. If the prior -0.41 result holds, breakeven at this level is confirmed net-negative.

Breakeven stop activates at +30% HWM. Symmetric with the -30% SL distance - the token must move up as much as the SL allows it to move down before breakeven engages. Only fires on trades that reach +30%, which is past the p75 (+26.7%) - so it protects only the genuine movers (roughly the top ~20-25% of trades by peak). Lowest false-exit rate among the engaging variants; minimal interference with the normal noise band. Hypothesis: best balance - protects the give-it-all-back tail on real movers without prematurely stopping the noise-band winners. If any breakeven level beats v25, this or +40 is the most likely.

Breakeven stop activates at +40% HWM. Latest engagement / least protective variant - only the strong movers (40%+ peak, ~p80-p85 of the distribution) get the breakeven floor. Almost no false exits (a trade that hits +40% rarely round-trips to entry without us noticing), but also protects the fewest trades. Effectively a near-control: behaves like v25 on the bulk of trades, only differing on the subset that pumps 40%+ then fully reverses. Useful as the upper anchor of the activation sweep - if even +40 underperforms v25, breakeven adds no value at any level; if +40 ~ v25 but +10 is much worse, it confirms the false-exit cost rises sharply as activation drops.
